title: Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens
intro: Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens, also known as the alpine poppy, is a variant of the alpine poppy plant (Papaver alpinum), which is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as reduce pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens include alkaloids, such as papaverine and thebaine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens include dried flower extracts, capsules, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens is a variety of the species Papaver alpinum. It belongs to the family Papaveraceae, commonly known as the poppy family. Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens is a flowering plant native to the alpine regions of Europe. The classification of Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Its classification also reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its alpine habitat.
benefits: Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens is a variant of the alpine poppy, which is known for its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts may have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin. Additionally, Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens is said to have antiseptic and antibacterial effects, making it a useful remedy for minor cuts and wounds. Its alkaloids may also possess antioxidant properties, protecting against cell damage and promoting overall well-being. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's health benefits and potential applications.

parts: Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens is a subspecies of the alpine poppy plant. The medicinal parts of Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens are its seeds and flowers, which contain alkaloids like papaverine and meconic acid. These alkaloids have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, inflammation, and insomnia. The plant's seeds, in particular, are known for their sedative and antispasmodic properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Papaver alpinum lusus glabrescens.
